Africa' label on that flip correctly identifies it as being minted at the Pretoria Mint in South Africa, which produced coins for India during World War II.

items.description: 1 Pice (often referred to as 1/4 Anna) issued by George VI (British India). Period: 1944. Mint: Bombay (Mumbai). Catalog: KM# 553.
notes: Obverse: Features the legend 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' around a central hole, with crown and decorative floral patterns.
Reverse: Central hole surrounded by the legend 'ONE PICE', 'INDIA', and the year '1944'. The denomination is also written in Hindi, Urdu, and Telugu.

This 'holed' pice was introduced during World War II (1943-1947) to conserve copper for the war effort. While the holder marks it as '1/4 Anna', the coin is officially denominated as 'One Pice' (which is equal in value to 1/4 Anna). The Bombay mint is identified by a small dot under the date.

items.title: 1 Pice - George VI, King Emperor
items.description: 1 Pice issued by George VI, King Emperor. Period: 1944. Mint: Calcutta (No mint mark). Catalog: KM# 533.
notes: Obverse: Features a central hole with an imperial crown at the top and the legend 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' around the perimeter.
Reverse: Central hole surrounded by an ornate floral wreath; contains the denomination '1 PICE' in English, Hindi, Urdu, and Bengali, with the year '1944' at the bottom.

This 'holed' or 'washer' type coin was designed during World War II to reduce the amount of metal used, conserving copper for the war effort. The 1944 Calcutta minting is one of the most common issues of this series.

items.title: 1 Pice - George VI / British India
items.description: 1 Pice issued by George VI / British India. Period: 1944 AD. Mint: Pretoria Mint, South Africa. Catalog: KM# 533.
notes: Obverse: Features a central hole surrounded by a stylized floral wreath. The inscription 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' is positioned around the hole.
Reverse: Central hole with the denomination '1 PICE' above and 'INDIA' with the date '1944' below. The value is also written in four Indian scripts: Devanagari, Urdu, Bengali, and Telugu.

This distinctive 'holed' or 'washer-type' coin was introduced in 1943 to conserve metal during World War II. Due to the war effort, the Pretoria Mint in South Africa was contracted to help meet the massive demand for small change in India. Pretoria mint issues for 1944 are distinguished by the absence of a mint mark and specific die characteristics.

items.title: One Pice - George VI, British India
items.description: One Pice issued by George VI, British India. Period: 1944. Mint: Lahore. Catalog: KM# 533.
notes: Obverse: Features a central hole with a crown at the top. The legend 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' and the date '1944' are positioned around the hole. A floral wreath surrounds the central design.
Reverse: Features a central hole. The denomination '1 PICE' and 'ONE PICE' are written in English, Hindi, Urdu, and Bengali around the hole. A decorative floral border circles the design.

This coin is known as the 'washer' pice or holed pice, introduced during World War II to save bronze for the war effort. The Lahore mint mark 'L' is typically found below the date on the obverse. While common, they are a significant part of WWII-era Indian numismatics.

items.title: 1 Pice - George VI, King Emperor
items.description: 1 Pice issued by George VI, King Emperor. Period: 1945. Mint: Bombay (Mumbai). Catalog: KM #533.
notes: Obverse: Features a central hole surrounded by a decorative wreath and the inscription 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' around the edge.

Reverse: Features a central hole with '1 PICE' at the top, the year '1945' at the bottom (with a dot mint mark below it for Bombay), and the denomination written in Hindi and Urdu on the left and right sides respectively.

The holed 1 Pice design was introduced during World War II (1943–1947) specifically to save copper and bronze for the war effort. These coins are extremely common but historically significant as 'war-time' issues. The details provided on the coin holder (1945, Bombay, Bronze, 1.98g) match the standard numismatic specs for this type.

items.title: 1 Pice - George VI
items.description: 1 Pice issued by George VI. Period: 1945. Mint: Calcutta (No mint mark). Catalog: KM# 533.
notes: Obverse: Legend 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' surrounding a central hole, with a crown at the top.
Reverse: Legend '1 PICE INDIA' and the date '1945' surrounding a central hole. The value is also written in Hindi (Ek Paisa) and Urdu (Ek Paisa) scripts.
Commonly known as the 'washer' coin due to its central hole, which was designed to save metal during World War II. The lack of a mint mark below the date indicates it was minted in Calcutta.

items.title: 1 Pice - King George VI, British India
items.description: 1 Pice issued by King George VI, British India. Period: 1945 AD. Mint: Lahore. Catalog: KM #533.
notes: Obverse: Features the legend 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' around a central circular hole, with a crown at the 12 o'clock position.
Reverse: Features '1 PICE' above the hole, 'INDIA' and '1945' below. Flanked by Hindi and Urdu translations of the denomination, with a decorative floral wreath around the hole.

This is a 'washer-type' coin, minted with a hole to conserve copper during World War II. The Lahore mint is identified by a small 'L' mint mark below the date. The coin shown is in a standard protective flip with handwritten collector notes.

items.title: 1 Pice - George VI (British India)
items.description: 1 Pice issued by George VI (British India). Period: 1947. Mint: Bombay (Mumbai). Catalog: KM #533.
notes: Obverse: Features a central hole with the inscription 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' around the perimeter, accompanied by a crown at the top and stylized floral patterns.
Reverse: Central hole surrounded by the legend '1 PICE INDIA' and the date '1947'. The denomination is also indicated in Devanagari and Urdu scripts at the top and bottom.

Commonly referred to as the 'Holed Pice' or 'Washer Pice', this design was utilized from 1943 to 1947 to conserve metal during World War II. 1947 is the final year of issue for this series before India's independence. The 'Bombay' mint is typically identified by a small dot under the date on the reverse.

items.title: 1 Pice - George VI
items.description: 1 Pice issued by George VI. Period: 1947. Mint: Calcutta. Catalog: KM# 533.
notes: Obverse: The legend 'GEORGE VI KING EMPEROR' inscribed around a large central hole.
Reverse: The denomination '1 PICE', 'INDIA', and the date '1947' around a central hole, all enclosed within a decorative floral vine border.

This is a common coin from the final year of the British Raj. The 'hole' design was introduced in 1943 to conserve bronze during World War II. Coins without a mint mark below the date were minted in Calcutta.
